design_philosophy.core_loop: Divide land into zones, build infrastructure, manage finances via taxation, observe emergent city development as Sims respond to conditions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing urban growth against resource constraints, economic viability, and environmental/infrastructure management
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Design and nurture a thriving city from conception, watching it develop organically based on player decisions and systems interactions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately eschews arcade/action elements in favor of systems-driven gameplay; emphasizes non-linear creative expression and emergent complexity over traditional win/lose conditions; positions player as city planner rather than action hero; gameplay serves as instruction in urban planning, economics, and politics

difficulty.checkpointing: Persistent city state; player can save and load
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency—player controls tax rates, zoning, infrastructure investment, and disaster frequency; scenarios provide optional time-limited challenges with win/loss conditions for players seeking structured difficulty

progression.upgrade_system: City development through residential, commercial, industrial zone expansion; reward buildings unlocked via scenario wins (mayor's mansion, casino in SNES+)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Scenarios gate access to specific city models and reward structures; freeplay mode offers unrestricted progression
replayability_hooks: Multiple scenarios with distinct starting conditions and objectives
Freeplay mode with no explicit goals enabling emergent storytelling
Different disaster event types create variable challenge conditions
Optimization challenge: player can retry scenarios to achieve better performance

world_building_techniques: Scenario briefings establish fictional or historical city contexts
Real-world city layouts replicated in scenario design
Emergent narrative through player observation of city evolution and NPC (Sim) behavior patterns
Disaster events create contextual drama and challenge arcs

faction_indicators: Tax policy creating wealth distribution tension
danger_signals: Uncontrolled crime levels affecting property values
Disaster events (earthquakes, tornadoes, floods, monster attacks)
Power grid failure causing cascading infrastructure problems
environmental_storytelling: Slums emerge adjacent to pollution sources (power plants, industry)
Population density and building types reflect player zoning decisions
